description: Per-interaction embodiment loop for OS-011 &body.os: perceive the host state (cwd, env, fs snapshot, process list), enumerate policy-filtered affordances, execute one typed action (shell_exec, file_write, file_delete, process_spawn, process_signal, etc.) with destructive-edge authorization, record the resulting TraceEdge, emit SurpriseSignal on forward-model prediction error. Every destructive edge MUST carry a fresh authorization block at both record and replay time (OS-011 §4.4).

phases.description: Capture the current OS state — cwd, env summary, filesystem entries with content hashes, process list — as an EnvironmentObservation. Volatile fields (perceived_at, raw pids, cursor state) are excluded from the replay-stable subset.

Enumerate typed actions currently do-able, run each through &govern.identity policy, partition into allowed vs policy_filtered_out. Most OS actions are destructive; non-destructive actions are limited to file_read and screen_capture.

Execute exactly one TypedAction. Invariants: I1 perceive-before-act, I2 affordance-bounded, I4 deterministic state encoding. Destructive actions (shell_exec, file_write, file_edit, file_delete, keyboard_input, mouse_click, mouse_drag, process_spawn, process_signal) require a fresh authorization block per OS-011 §4.4.
Assemble the TraceEdge with full provenance (capability: &body.os). Emit SurpriseSignal if the forward-model prediction diverged from the observed state_after.
Finalize the trace at session end, submit to &memory.episodic.store. Successful traces become candidates for crystallization into FleetPrompt SkillCandidates (see fleet.skill_crystallizations).

substrates.name: host_os
substrates.note: Typically driven via Claude Computer Use or OpenClaw. Sandbox (containers, jails, restricted fs) is strongly recommended. The Simulator backend swaps this out for deterministic fixtures.

substrates.name: delegatic
substrates.note: Issues fresh authorization blocks for destructive edges. Required for every replay of a destructive action — OS-011 §4.4 prohibits prior-authorization carry.

consumes.note: Replay payload returned by retrieve(action: replay).
Fresh authorization issued per destructive replay edge.

reference_implementations.name: body-os (Simulator backend)
reference_implementations.conformance: OS-011 v0.1 §9 — 12/12 tests pass against BodyOs.Backend.Simulator

reference_implementations.name: body-os (ComputerUse backend)
reference_implementations.note: Adapter shell for Claude Computer Use / OpenClaw; subprocess wiring pending.
